claim: No Stable, release, conformance, manual assistive-technology, package-parity, or publication claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.provider: Context-only; nested providers always reuse the nearest stack and document listener.
semantics.application: A neutral application boundary that becomes natively inert while an external modal layer is active.
semantics.layer: A neutral wrapper or one consumer element; overlay roles, names, focus trap, and focus return remain the owning component's contract.

stack.order: Registration order defines indices, the top layer, and the actual portal-root visual z-order when Layer composes asChild.
stack.escape: Only the top layer is considered; if dismissible it receives one escape detail, otherwise lower layers remain unchanged.
stack.inert: Every registered application root outside the top environment-managed modal is inert until the final such modal unregisters; external behavior owners may opt out without leaving the shared stack.
stack.scroll: For environment-managed modals, the manager records scroll coordinates and every touched body style, compensates the scrollbar, and restores exact prior values. Externally managed modals do not duplicate these effects.
stack.cleanup: Layer, root, key listener, inert state, and scroll lock all clean up under Strict Mode; unchanged native modal ownership is not torn down for unrelated non-modal stack changes.
stack.environmentOptOut: manageEnvironment=false omits inerting, body styles, scroll locking, and cleanup while preserving shared stack and Escape order; modal=false also omits modal membership.

consumerResponsibilities: Portal modal layers outside LayerManager.Application so the modal is never inside an inert ancestor.
Use a dedicated overlay primitive for role, accessible name, initial focus, trapping, dismissal policy, and focus restoration.
Register every non-modal application root that must become inert.
Do not use visual z-index values to imply a stack order different from registration order.
